key_topics: Operations and Algebraic Thinking: addition and subtraction within 20
Word problems involving addition and subtraction within 20
Properties of addition: commutative and associative
Relationship between addition and subtraction
Counting to 120, starting from any number
Number and Operations in Base Ten: tens and ones
Comparing two-digit numbers using <, >, =
Adding and subtracting within 100 using place value
Mentally adding 10 or subtracting 10 from two-digit numbers
Measurement and Data: ordering objects by length, measuring with non-standard units
Telling time to the hour and half-hour
Representing and interpreting data (charts, graphs)
Geometry: distinguishing shapes (2D and 3D), partitioning circles and rectangles into halves and quarters
competences: Add and subtract within 20 using strategies (counting on, making 10, decomposing)
Understand place value for two-digit numbers (tens and ones)
Compare two two-digit numbers using symbols <, >, =
Tell and write time in hours and half-hours using analog and digital clocks
Partition circles and rectangles into two and four equal shares
typical_mistakes: Counting all instead of counting on for addition
Reversing tens and ones when writing two-digit numbers
Confusing half-hour (30 minutes) with quarter-hour (15 minutes) on clocks
Not recognizing that 2+3 and 3+2 are the same (commutativity)

key_topics: Reading Literature: key ideas, characters, settings, events in stories
Comparing and contrasting adventures of characters in stories
Reading Informational Text: main topic and key details
Asking and answering questions about key details
Phonics: consonant digraphs (ch, sh, th, wh), long vowel patterns
Decoding regularly spelled one-syllable words
High-frequency (sight) words
Reading with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension
Writing: opinion pieces, informative texts, narrative writing
Using spacing between words when writing
Speaking and Listening: participating in collaborative conversations
Language: using nouns and verbs, subject-verb agreement
competences: Read first-grade text with purpose and understanding
Decode words using phonics and word analysis skills
Write simple opinion, informative, and narrative texts
Participate in collaborative conversations with peers and adults
Use correct grammar in written and spoken English
typical_mistakes: Omitting silent letters in high-frequency words (said, was, the)
Writing sentences without capital letters or end marks
Confusing long and short vowel sounds

key_topics: Light and Sound: properties, how objects can be seen only when illuminated
Communication using light and sound
Structure and Function of plants and animals
Mimicry and camouflage as survival adaptations
Parent and offspring similarities and differences
Weather and climate: patterns, seasonal change
Objects in the sky: sun, moon, stars
competences: Plan and conduct investigations about light and sound
Identify structures that help plants and animals survive
Use weather observations to describe patterns
typical_mistakes: Thinking all animals look exactly like their parents (not understanding variation)

key_topics: Families and communities: roles, rules, responsibilities
School and classroom community
Maps and globes: basic geography skills
American symbols and traditions: flag, Pledge of Allegiance
National holidays: Veterans Day, Thanksgiving, MLK Day
Goods and services, needs and wants
Past and present: how communities change over time
competences: Identify rules and responsibilities in family and school
Locate places on a simple map
Explain the significance of national symbols and holidays
